name: Prior Authorization Forensics (PAF) v1
description: Adjudicate fictional health-insurance claim dossiers by reconciling plan rules, service schedules, riders, provider-network notes, prior authorization/referral correspondence, date windows, and accumulators.
determinism_notes: The generator is seeded. The public dossier for each item contains all adjudication rules and evidence needed to derive the answer.

closest_existing_evals.name: Reimbursement Forensics (BenchBench)
closest_existing_evals.why_close: Both require exact numeric decisions from messy policy and transaction evidence.
closest_existing_evals.why_not_duplicate: PAF uses medical benefit adjudication, denial precedence, network constraints, referral/auth validity, accumulators, and plan riders rather than travel receipts and exchange-rate reimbursement.

closest_existing_evals.name: DocVQA / document-understanding evals
closest_existing_evals.why_close: The solver must read semi-structured documents and extract relevant facts.
closest_existing_evals.why_not_duplicate: PAF is not visual extraction or open QA; it is deterministic cross-document rule application with a closed answer schema.

closest_existing_evals.name: Legal/contract rule-following benchmarks
closest_existing_evals.why_close: The benchmark includes rider precedence and policy exceptions.
closest_existing_evals.why_not_duplicate: The final output is an auditable claim calculation, not a subjective legal conclusion or a clean amendment puzzle.
